stories.headline: Congress Approves Temporary Homeland Security Funding Amid Partisan Clash
stories.summary: The US Senate passed a temporary funding deal for the Department of Homeland Security early Friday, but House Republicans pushed back immediately. Speaker Mike Johnson accused Democrats of political gamesmanship as the DHS shutdown entered its sixth week, with TSA workers facing payment uncertainties that prompted a presidential executive order to ensure their compensation.

stories.headline: Bank of America Settles Epstein-Related Lawsuit for $72.5 Million
stories.summary: Bank of America agreed to pay $72.5 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the bank financially benefited from Jeffrey Epstein's circle and failed to file suspicious activity reports despite red flags in connected accounts. The settlement follows similar actions against JPMorgan and Deutsche Bank, as regulators continue scrutinizing financial institutions' compliance with anti-money laundering obligations.

stories.headline: Trump Signs Executive Order Ensuring TSA Pay During DHS Shutdown
stories.summary: President Trump issued an executive order directing federal officials to ensure TSA employees receive payment during the ongoing Department of Homeland Security shutdown, which he characterized as reaching a breaking point. The move came after six weeks of funding lapse that threatened airport security operations and worker morale across the country's transportation system.

stories.headline: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Rises to 57.0 in March, Beating Expectations
stories.summary: Final University of Michigan consumer sentiment for March came in at 57.0, up from February's 54.0 and beating consensus estimates of 53.5. The improvement reflects consumers reacting positively to signs of inflation moderation and continued labor market stability, though sentiment remains historically subdued amid geopolitical uncertainty from the Iran conflict.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Gains Only 0.49% Year-to-Date as Market Mood Darkens
stories.summary: The S&P 500 has managed just a 0.49% gain in 2026, a stark contrast to the bullish momentum of late 2025. Investor caution stems from the Iran-US conflict that began in late February, slowing GDP growth at 1.4% annualized in Q4 2025, and persistent inflation pressures that have kept the Fed from cutting rates as aggressively as markets hoped.

stories.headline: Fed's Barr and Williams Signal Patience on Rate Cuts Amid Inflation Persistence
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Barr and NY Fed President Williams delivered speeches emphasizing a patient approach to monetary policy, citing sticky inflation and geopolitical risks. The 6-month Treasury bill auction settled at 3.63%, reflecting market expectations that the Fed will hold rates steady through mid-2026 as policymakers balance growth concerns against price stability.

stories.headline: Sanders and AOC Introduce AI Data Center Moratorium Bill
stories.summary: Sen. Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced the Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act to pause new AI data center construction until safeguards ensure worker benefits, environmental protection, and community safety. The bill responds to growing concerns over AI's massive energy demands, with data centers projected to consume up to 12% of US electricity by 2028, straining local grids and water supplies.

stories.headline: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video Generator to Focus on 'Spud' Model
stories.summary: OpenAI discontinued its Sora AI video generation tool on March 25 to redirect compute resources toward 'Spud,' its next major foundation model expected within weeks. The decision reflects OpenAI's strategic pivot in the intensifying AI race, prioritizing general-purpose model capabilities over specialized tools as competition with Anthropic and Google DeepMind heats up.

stories.headline: Meta Releases TRIBE v2 Brain Prediction Model Trained on 500+ Hours of fMRI Data
stories.summary: Meta launched TRIBE v2, a foundation model trained on over 500 hours of fMRI data from 700+ participants that can predict human brain responses to visual and auditory stimuli. Built on Meta's Algonauts 2025-winning architecture, the model enables zero-shot predictions across subjects, languages, and tasks, advancing neuroscience-AI integration with open code and an interactive demo.

stories.headline: Wikipedia Bans AI-Generated Articles After Quality Crisis
stories.summary: Wikipedia implemented a formal ban on AI-generated articles following a surge of low-quality, hallucinated content flooding the platform. The policy shift came as AI writing tools proliferated and volunteer editors struggled to keep pace with submissions containing fabricated citations and factual errors, threatening the encyclopedia's credibility as a knowledge resource.

stories.headline: Figure's Humanoid Robot Demonstrates at White House
stories.summary: Figure AI showcased its humanoid robot at the White House in a high-profile demonstration of embodied AI capabilities. The appearance came as the administration explores robotics for manufacturing and defense applications, with Figure having raised over $1.5 billion to date and the broader humanoid robotics market attracting unprecedented investment from companies like Tesla, Amazon, and Microsoft.

stories.headline: ChatGPT Uninstalls Surge 295% After OpenAI's Pentagon Deal
stories.summary: US uninstalls of the ChatGPT mobile app jumped 295% day-over-day following news of OpenAI's deal with the Department of Defense, reflecting growing public backlash over AI-military partnerships. The consumer revolt highlights the tension between AI companies' commercial expansion into government contracts and their user bases' expectations around ethical AI deployment.

stories.headline: US Startup Funding Slows Sharply in March to $13B Amid Iran War Uncertainty
stories.summary: American startups raised just $13 billion in seed through growth-stage funding so far in March, a sharp decline from January and February's record highs. The slowdown is primarily driven by investor caution following the Iran conflict that began February 28, which triggered stock market volatility and dampened appetite for risk assets despite a partial market rebound.

stories.headline: Global VC Hit Record $189B in February as AI Megarounds Dominate
stories.summary: A record $189 billion in global venture capital flowed to startups in February, with AI companies capturing $171 billion — led by OpenAI, Anthropic, and Waymo. The concentration of capital in just three companies underscores the winner-take-most dynamics in AI funding, as institutional investors rush to back perceived category leaders at increasingly massive valuations.

stories.headline: General Catalyst Targets $10B Fund as VCs Race to Deploy AI Capital
stories.summary: General Catalyst is raising a $10 billion fund, following Thrive Capital's $10 billion close and Andreessen Horowitz's $15 billion raise in January, with Founders Fund nearing $6 billion. The fundraising surge reflects massive dry powder accumulation and VC conviction that 2026 will see transformative AI deployments, even as March's US funding slowdown suggests near-term deployment caution.

stories.headline: Wayve Raises Record $1.2B Series D for Autonomous Driving
stories.summary: UK-based autonomous driving startup Wayve secured $1.2 billion in Series D funding backed by Mercedes-Benz and Stellantis, making it one of Europe's largest startup rounds ever. The investment reflects automakers' strategic shift toward partnering with AI-native companies for self-driving technology rather than building in-house, as Europe positions itself as a competitor in the autonomous vehicle race.

stories.headline: Manifold Raises $8M for AI Agent Security as Seed Deals Hold Steady
stories.summary: Manifold raised $8 million and Obin AI secured $7 million in the week of March 17-24, showing that early-stage AI deals remain resilient even as overall US funding decelerates. Manifold's focus on securing AI agents reflects growing enterprise demand for safety tooling as autonomous AI systems proliferate in production environments across financial services and healthcare.

stories.headline: Plaid Completes Liquidity Round at $8B Valuation, Down from Peak
stories.summary: Fintech infrastructure company Plaid completed an employee liquidity round at an $8 billion valuation, reflecting the broader fintech valuation reset from pandemic-era highs. The round provides employee liquidity while signaling that even established fintech leaders face tighter investor scrutiny and a market that increasingly rewards profitability and sustainable unit economics over growth at all costs.

stories.headline: March 2026 Marks 'Tipping Point' for Global EV Adoption as Oil Crisis Accelerates Shift
stories.summary: Industry data suggests March 2026 is the tipping point for electric vehicle adoption over internal combustion engines, driven by a war-induced oil crisis from Hormuz Strait disruptions causing fuel price spikes. With battery prices down 90% since 2010, doubled EV range, and running costs roughly 10x cheaper per kilometer via efficient motors and cheap solar electricity, analysts predict rapid ICE collapse through 2026-2027.

stories.headline: Natural Gas Dominates US Power at 40% as Oil Remains Geopolitical Vulnerability
stories.summary: Natural gas continues to account for 40% of US electricity generation in 2026, cementing its role as the backbone of the domestic power grid even as renewables expand. However, oil remains the critical geopolitical vulnerability through chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz, creating a paradox where America's energy security at home contrasts sharply with its exposure to global oil supply disruptions.

stories.headline: IEA Warns of Energy Crisis as Iran Conflict Disrupts Global Supply Chains
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency issued a forecast warning of an escalating energy crisis as the US-Iran conflict disrupts oil tanker routes through the Strait of Hormuz. The warning comes as global oil prices have spiked due to supply uncertainties, forcing European and Asian nations to accelerate emergency stockpile releases and diversify energy sourcing away from Middle Eastern dependence.

stories.headline: West Virginia Passes Comprehensive Energy Plan Targeting 50 GW by 2050
stories.summary: West Virginia's House Bill 5381 creates the Comprehensive Energy Policy and Development Plan Act of 2026, tasking the Office of Energy with crafting a strategy to boost capacity from 16 to 50 gigawatts by 2050. Governor Morrisey's '50 by 50' plan positions the state as the East Coast's energy 'battery,' balancing fossil fuels with grid stability amid debates over baseload versus renewables priorities.

stories.headline: Trump Pulls Back from Iran Airstrikes, Easing Oil Market Fears Temporarily
stories.summary: President Trump decided against direct airstrikes on Iranian territory, providing temporary relief to oil markets that had been pricing in potential full-scale conflict. The decision reflects a calculated approach to avoid further oil supply disruption while maintaining pressure through sanctions and naval presence, though analysts warn the underlying geopolitical tensions remain unresolved.

stories.headline: Enterprise Products Partners Sees Surge in Institutional Investment
stories.summary: Exchange Traded Concepts increased its stake in midstream giant Enterprise Products Partners by 7.7% in Q4 2025, bringing holdings to nearly $30.5 million as disclosed in a March 28 SEC filing. The increased institutional interest reflects confidence in US oil and gas infrastructure assets as energy security concerns from the Iran conflict drive demand for domestic pipeline and export terminal capacity.

stories.headline: Geopolitical Shock Exposes Critical Vulnerabilities in US Healthcare Supply Chains
stories.summary: The ongoing Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruptions have exposed structural vulnerabilities in US pharmaceutical and medical equipment supply chains, with nearly half of generic drugs relying on Gulf-linked energy and petrochemicals for production. Healthcare providers are scrambling to buffer inventory, diversify sourcing, and establish alternative logistics routes as the conflict threatens medication availability.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Launches Major Crackdown on $3.7B in Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
stories.summary: The administration announced sweeping anti-fraud measures including deferring $259.5 million in Minnesota Medicaid funding, a nationwide DMEPOS supplier moratorium, and revoking billing rights for 5,586 providers amid $3.7 billion in fraud referrals. The crackdown uses data-driven strategies to proactively identify questionable claims, signaling an aggressive approach to healthcare cost containment under HHS Secretary Kennedy and CMS Administrator Oz.

stories.headline: Congress Extends COVID-Era Medicare Telehealth Flexibilities Through 2027
stories.summary: The Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2026 preserved expanded telehealth coverage including home-based visits, waived geographic restrictions, audio-only services, and delayed mental health in-person requirements through 2027. The extension provides critical stability for virtual care delivery as lawmakers work toward permanent telehealth reforms, following years of disruptive short-term extensions that created uncertainty for providers and patients.

stories.headline: Big Beautiful Bill Ends Medicaid Expansion Incentives, Threatens Coverage for 10 Million
stories.summary: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act's provisions ending fiscal incentives for Medicaid expansion could cause 10 million Americans to lose coverage by 2034, while non-extension of ACA premium tax credits may add 14 million to the uninsured rolls per CBO estimates. The legislation reshapes healthcare access starting in 2026 as part of broader federal spending constraints, with medical students also facing reduced loan options.

stories.headline: MedPAC Recommends Flat 2027 Hospital Payments Plus $1B Safety-Net Boost
stories.summary: The Medicare Payment Advisory Commission's March 2026 report to Congress proposes maintaining current payment rates for 2027 inpatient and outpatient services while adding a $1 billion safety-net index for vulnerable hospitals. The recommendations come amid growing financial pressure on hospitals from rising uninsured populations and cost inflation, with the hospital industry pushing for higher payment updates.

stories.headline: MACPAC Pushes Wage Transparency for Medicaid Home Care Workers
stories.summary: The Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission recommended increasing wage transparency for home and community-based services workers in its March 2026 report, addressing chronic workforce shortages in the sector. The push comes as aging demographics increase demand for home care services while low wages and poor visibility into compensation structures make recruitment and retention increasingly difficult.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Secures First-Ever FDA Approval for Longevity Gene Therapy Trial
stories.summary: Life Biosciences obtained FDA clearance for the first human clinical trial of its epigenetic reprogramming therapy ER-100, targeting age-related vision loss in glaucoma patients. The breakthrough builds on David Sinclair's Harvard research using three Yamanaka factors to rejuvenate cells without losing function or risking tumor formation, marking the historic transition from laboratory aging reversal to human clinical testing.

stories.headline: Harvard's Sinclair Declares Aging Reversal Testing Begins in Humans at World Government Summit
stories.summary: David Sinclair announced at the World Governments Summit 2026 that humanity is poised to test aging reversal and disease cures for the first time in history. The declaration builds on his lab's validated epigenetic reprogramming work using partial Yamanaka factors that have restored youthful cell states in animal models, with the first human trials now underway through Life Biosciences.

stories.headline: Longevity Science Crosses Clinical Threshold with Senolytics and Epigenetic Clocks
stories.summary: GESDA's 2026 Radar Spotlight highlights longevity science crossing into clinical practice via small molecules targeting senescent 'zombie' cells and epigenetic clocks measuring biological age through DNA methylation patterns. The shift was propelled by Zurich University's validation of Omega-3 interventions slowing biological clocks and sleep augmentation studies showing measurable healthspan improvements.

stories.headline: Stem Cell Therapy Shows Promise for Aging Frailty in Phase 2b Trial
stories.summary: A randomized phase 2b dose-escalation trial of laromestrocel stem cell therapy demonstrated significant improvements in aging frailty outcomes, according to the February 2026 Rejuvenation Roundup. The findings add to growing evidence that cell-based therapies can address fundamental aging processes, alongside studies showing lifetime cognitive enrichment delays dementia onset by 5-7 years.

stories.headline: Los Angeles Emerges as Longevity Industry Hub with Biotech and AI Diagnostics
stories.summary: Los Angeles is positioning itself as a major longevity industry center, with a growing cluster of biotech startups, AI diagnostics companies, and wellness technology firms turning aging into an investable market. The city's convergence of entertainment, tech, and healthcare talent creates unique advantages for longevity companies seeking both consumer adoption and clinical validation of anti-aging interventions.

stories.headline: Manulife Launches Global Longevity Institute with $350M Commitment Through 2030
stories.summary: Insurance giant Manulife launched its Global Longevity Institute with a $350 million commitment through 2030 to accelerate progress on lifelong health and financial resilience. The investment signals mainstream financial services firms recognizing that extending healthspan creates both societal value and commercial opportunity in insurance products, retirement planning, and preventive health services.

stories.headline: SpaceX Launches Starlink 373 with 25 Satellites from Vandenberg on March 26
stories.summary: SpaceX successfully launched 25 Starlink satellites to low-Earth orbit from Vandenberg Space Force Base on March 26, with booster B1081 completing its 23rd flight and landing on the 'Of Course I Still Love You' droneship. The mission continues SpaceX's relentless Starlink deployment cadence as the company races to build out its broadband constellation to serve growing global demand.

stories.headline: SpaceX Hits 600th Starlink Satellite Launch of 2026 in Just Three Months
stories.summary: SpaceX reached the milestone of launching its 600th Starlink satellite of 2026 during a March 20 mission from Cape Canaveral, putting the company on pace for over 2,400 satellite deployments this year. The unprecedented launch cadence reflects SpaceX's mature Falcon 9 reusability platform, with individual boosters now routinely flying 20+ missions and turnaround times shortened to days.

stories.headline: SpaceX Schedules Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission from Vandenberg for Late March
stories.summary: SpaceX has scheduled its Transporter-16 rideshare mission from SLC-4E at Vandenberg, offering affordable access to sun-synchronous orbit for small satellite operators and government payloads. The Transporter program has become a cornerstone of the small-sat launch market, enabling startups and research institutions to reach orbit at a fraction of the cost of dedicated launches.

stories.headline: NASA SpaceX CRS-34 Resupply Mission to ISS Upcoming
stories.summary: NASA's Commercial Resupply Services mission CRS-34 is scheduled for launch aboard a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft, continuing the regular cadence of science experiments and supplies to the International Space Station. The mission carries critical research payloads as NASA prepares for the ISS's final operational years before transitioning to commercial space stations in the late 2020s.

stories.headline: Firefly Aerospace Blue Ghost Mission 2 CLPS Lunar Lander in Preparation
stories.summary: Firefly Aerospace is preparing its Blue Ghost Mission 2 under NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services program, aiming to deliver science instruments to the Moon's surface. The mission represents the growing maturation of commercial lunar delivery services as NASA relies increasingly on private companies to support Artemis-era lunar exploration and science objectives.

stories.headline: Global Launch Cadence Accelerates with Multiple Falcon 9 Missions Weekly
stories.summary: SpaceX is maintaining an average of 2-3 Falcon 9 launches per week in March 2026, dominating the global launch manifest with missions from both Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg. The cadence demonstrates SpaceX's industrial-scale launch operations, with the company's reusable rocket fleet enabling a frequency that was unimaginable just five years ago and putting pressure on competitors like Rocket Lab and Arianespace.

stories.headline: NFL Pushes to Renegotiate $110B TV Deals Before 2026 Season
stories.summary: The NFL is pushing to renegotiate its massive $110 billion domestic broadcast agreements with CBS, NBC, Fox, ESPN, and Amazon before the 2026 season, despite contracts not expiring until 2032. The league believes its content value has surged following the NBA's $76 billion deal and recent media consolidations like Paramount's WBD acquisition, and plans to use a 'change of control' clause to initiate talks.

stories.headline: NFL Demands $1B More from CBS in 50% Rights Fee Increase
stories.summary: The NFL has specifically targeted Paramount's CBS Sports for a 50% fee increase worth approximately $1 billion annually for AFC Sunday afternoon games. The demand is backed by CBS's record 2025 viewership averaging 21.25 million viewers (up 11%), combined with Paramount's deepening sports investment through its $7.6 billion UFC deal, setting the tone for broader renegotiations with all partners.

stories.headline: MLB Launches New Three-Year Media Deals with ESPN, NBCUniversal, and Netflix
stories.summary: Major League Baseball secured fresh 2026-2028 media agreements bringing NBC back to baseball broadcasts after 26 years with Sunday Night Baseball, while Netflix lands Opening Night (Yankees-Giants) and the Home Run Derby. The deals expand streaming access through MLB.TV via ESPN and leverage stars like Shohei Ohtani and Aaron Judge to grow the sport's national audience.

stories.headline: Paramount Secures $7.7B UFC Streaming Deal, Doubling ESPN's Prior Rights
stories.summary: Paramount locked in exclusive seven-year UFC rights for $7.7 billion ($1.1 billion annually), shifting the franchise from ESPN's $550 million per year deal to Paramount+ subscriptions and CBS events. The move reflects the broader industry pivot from pay-per-view and traditional broadcast toward streaming subscriptions as the primary distribution model for premium combat sports content.

stories.headline: Streamers to Spend $14.2B on Sports Rights in 2026, Up 7% Year-Over-Year
stories.summary: Streaming platforms including Amazon Prime Video are projected to invest $14.2 billion in sports rights in 2026, a 7% increase over 2025. The spending surge is driven by simultaneous renegotiations across NFL, NHL, and MLB, rising women's sports valuations with WNBA and NWSL sponsorships up 33%, and streamers' recognition that live sports remain the most effective subscriber acquisition tool.

stories.headline: Broadcast CEOs Push Consolidation as Essential to Compete for NFL Rights
stories.summary: Media industry executives argue that mergers like the pending Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery combination are vital for broadcasters to afford the NFL's renegotiated rights packages targeting $10 billion annually. Rising prices and new entrants like tech giants are forcing traditional broadcasters to consolidate for financial leverage, fundamentally reshaping the media landscape around sports content economics.
